summary: Patrick McKenzie reports his best month ever for Bingo Card Creator sales as of mid-March 2007, with 19 sales generating approximately $475 in gross income and $360 in profit. He attributes the success to organic growth and improvements to his AdWords campaign rather than any specific new initiative. He expresses optimism about potentially hitting his $1,000 monthly sales target in March instead of April. He also notes his next project is on hold while he searches for a day job.
key_points: Consistent daily sales replaced the usual feast-or-famine pattern, suggesting growing organic demand
AdWords campaign optimization and fixing demo bugs from the previous month contributed to improved sales
Operating expenses remained low at $108/month, with AdWords being the dominant cost at $90
The $1,000 monthly sales milestone was within reach, potentially a month ahead of schedule
His side project development was deprioritized in favor of day job searching
money_quotes: I'm having my best month ever.
Unlike most months the sales are not in fits and spurts — every day when I wake up its BAM! You've got money!
I attribute this success more to organic growth than anything I have done.
I think by the end of the month I will probably have in excess of $800 worth of sales and possibly, knock on wood, I will make my $1,000 sales target in March rather than in April.
Wow, its been 9 months already… where has the time gone.
key_insight: Consistent organic growth and small optimizations to paid acquisition can compound into milestone months without requiring dramatic new initiatives.
